More Notes: Negative value: decrease in reef accretion potential.
Slightly positive reef accretion on south crest.
Restoration could reduce total water levels under intermediate-to-high sea-level rise scenarios by up to 0.81 m on average.
Successful coral restoration could negate wave-driven component for low-end sea-level rise scenarios, maintaining balance with sea-level rise of 0.2-0.5 m by 2100.
The reef crest at BIRNM could exceed 5 mm per year in reef accretion under a scenario where A. palmata cover is increased by ~30%.
Under significant and successful restoration resulting in a +30% A. palmata cover, total water levels during storms could be reduced by >10% (0.81 m).
The reef could keep pace with a sea-level rise of ~0.5 m if successful restoration is achieved.
